session.task: Build AWSPrivateMCP — private MCP server on AWS

context.last_action: Created README.md, DEVELOPER.md, ARCHITECTURE.md and pushed to GitHub

state.problem: Need a private MCP server that keeps all thought data within AWS — no third-party API routing (replacing Supabase+OpenRouter Open Brain guide)
state.approach: Three-Lambda architecture: process-thought (core), ingest-thought (Slack), mcp-server (MCP protocol). S3 Vectors for storage, Bedrock for embeddings+classification, API Gateway for routing
next_steps: Test MCP tools in a new Claude Code session
Add Slack deduplication for 3-second retry issue

decisions: Named project AWSPrivateMCP — positioned as AWS-native tool for LinkedIn visibility
New dedicated AWS account (951921971435) in FeO org
Three-Lambda architecture with shared process-thought core for extensibility
S3 Vectors over DynamoDB brute-force — native similarity search, pay-per-query
Bedrock Titan v2 for embeddings (1024-dim), Claude 3 Haiku for classification
API Gateway API keys for MCP auth — simplest option for single-user tool
MCP SDK stateless mode + JSON response for Lambda compatibility
Express + @codegenie/serverless-express to bridge MCP SDK to API Gateway
Slack secrets via CDK context, not SSM or Secrets Manager
S3 Vectors bucket+index managed by CDK L1 constructs (not manually created)
Repo pattern follows badgerfy-infra: flat single-package, pnpm, infra+lambdas+types

aws_resources_created: API Gateway REST API with API key
